ref: After Jesus had sent the crowds away, he got into a boat and sailed across the lake. He came to shore near the town of Magadan.
text: Ka Yisa ŋɔn daa kyeoosi ba ka ba kyaŋŋi maa, ŋɔn ni u nyaandɔliba maa daa kpɛ nyariŋŋu n‑kyaŋŋi Magyada tiŋŋa ziiya.
translated: When Jesus and his disciples had left the crowd and gone inside, they asked him about the meaning of the parable.

ref: Every tree producing bad fruit will be chopped down and burned.
text: Tiseelli kam kan bu wanni walisuma, ba nin ŋmaai ka m‑basi, ka zaŋŋi ka n‑niŋi bugum ni.
translated: If you have a good thing, do not boast about it or show off, lest it turns into fire for you.

ref: Jesus said, “I was sent only to the people of Israel! They are like a flock of lost sheep.”
text: Ka u daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, ni, “Ba daa bu tum ma na faasee ni n kyɛ Izraɛli dima ban bɔrigri hoo peesi la saani na.”
translated: And he answered and said to them, "They did not need to come to me, but to the lost sheep of the house of Israel."

ref: If any place won't welcome you or listen to your message, leave and shake the dust from your feet as a warning to them.”
text: Ya yi ti paai tinseaa ka ba ku sakki n‑deaai ya, ka za'asi ya yɛttɔ'a wumpu, ya yim tiŋŋa maa ni ka kpaasi ya napɔna tankpoo m‑basi n‑niŋi tiŋŋa wa ni, ka lala nin leebi din wulisiri ba halibeoo ŋɔn nyɛ seem.”
translated: And when he reached a certain place and they did not receive him, he left that place and shook the dust from his feet as a testimony against them, and this became a sign showing what their judgment would be like.

ref: But you prove you really are the relatives of the ones who killed the prophets.
text: Lala zugu ka ya wulisiri ya mansi halibeoo, ni, ya badima daa nyɛ ninvugseeba ban daa ku anabidima maa.
translated: And at that time he made it known and revealed that you are the people who killed your prophets.
